sources.title: Palantir Decision Lineage (5D schema)
sources.summary: 5-dimensional Decision Lineage schema: WHEN/ATOP_WHICH/THROUGH_WHICH/BY_WHOM/WITH_WHAT, per Chief Architect Akshay Krishnaswamy blog.palantir.com Jan 2024

sources.title: Palantir Action Mutations — Two-Tier Architecture + Submission Criteria
sources.summary: Two-tier action architecture (declarative + function-backed, mutually exclusive), submission criteria (9 constraint types), validate action API, OSDK 2.0 $validateOnly/$returnEdits options

sources.title: Palantir Logic Functions — Edit Functions v2
sources.summary: Edit functions v2: must return Edits[] (not void), createEditBatch<OntologyEdit>(client) pattern, batch.getEdits() — authoring helper does NOT commit edits

sources.title: Palantir 6-Phase Validation Timeline
sources.summary: 6-phase validation: Design, Compile, Deploy, Merge, Runtime, Post-Write. Each catches distinct error classes.

sources.title: Palantir TypeScript as Grounding
sources.summary: TypeScript + OSDK codegen creates mechanically hallucination-proof pipeline. 5 schema categories: ObjectType, Property, LinkType, ActionType, Function.

sources.title: Anthropic Managed Agents — Brain/Session/Hands
sources.summary: Session = append-only event log (durable, immutable, server-side); Harness = stateless orchestrator (cattle); Sandbox = stateless executor (cattle)

sources.title: palantir-mini v0 Blueprint (this session synthesis)
sources.summary: v0 architecture synthesizing Managed Agents virtualization + Palantir 5 patterns + OMC best practices. Being refined this pipeline.

sources.title: Claude Code Hooks Reference (v2.1.108)
sources.summary: Official v2.1.108 hook input/output schemas. PreToolUse uses hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision (top-level 'decision' DEPRECATED). TaskCreated/TaskCompleted schemas verified. PreCompact block mechanism confirmed.

sources.title: Claude Code Plugins Reference (v2.1.108)
sources.summary: Plugin manifest fields, default file locations (.claude-plugin/plugin.json, skills/, agents/, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json, monitors/monitors.json, bin/), ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} vs ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}, plugin agent security restrictions (agents cannot define hooks/mcpServers/permissionMode)

sources.title: Palantir OSDK TypeScript v2 Ontology Edits
sources.summary: OSDK v2 Edits discriminated union: Edits.Object<T>, Edits.Interface<T>, Edits.Link<Src,'linkName'>. createEditBatch<OntologyEdit>(client) + batch.update/create/delete/link/unlink/getEdits.

sources.title: proper-lockfile npm package (atomic file lock)
sources.summary: mkdir-based atomic file lock. NFS-safe, unlike O_EXCL. 9.27M weekly downloads. Last published 2020.

claims.text: Decision Lineage captures 5 dimensions (WHEN/ATOP_WHICH/THROUGH_WHICH/BY_WHOM/WITH_WHAT) per decision artifact
Anthropic Managed Agents Session = append-only event log; Harness = stateless orchestrator; Sandbox = stateless executor
Session event log is durable, immutable and persists across disconnects
Three independent references (Anthropic MA, Palantir DL, OMC clawhip) converge on append-only log architecture
Palantir Two-Tier Action Architecture: Tier 1 declarative CRUD + Tier 2 function-backed, mutually exclusive
Edit Functions v2 return Edits[] without commit; commit only happens when wrapped as function-backed Action
Authoring helper executes edit functions without committing edits — explicit test-without-side-effects guarantee
Submission Criteria are independent from edit permissions; ALL must pass for action submission
Submission criteria support 9 constraint types: Range/ArraySize/StringLength/StringRegexMatch/OneOf/ObjectQueryResult/ObjectPropertyValue/GroupMember/Unevaluable
CC agent frontmatter supports disallowedTools as deny-list, enforced at tool invocation time
OSDK generates TypeScript types from Ontology metadata — only Ontology-defined concepts compile
Compile-time validation catches incompatible type changes after OSDK regen
palantir-math data.ts is 141618 bytes / 2190 LOC — exceeds OSDK 1.x single-file threshold
OSDK v2 Edits discriminated union has 3 variants: Edits.Object<T>, Edits.Interface<T>, Edits.Link<Src,'linkName'>
Plugin MCP servers auto-start when plugin is enabled; tools appear as mcp__<server>__<tool>
Palantir OSDK 2.0 supports $validateOnly:true — evaluates submission criteria without applying edits
Palantir OSDK 2.0 supports $returnEdits:true — executes and returns applied edits for post-commit audit
Palantir 6-phase validation: Design/Compile/Deploy/Merge/Runtime/Post-Write, each catching distinct error classes
CC v2.1.105 PreCompact supports decision:block — blocks compaction when ontology invariants fail
CC v2.1.84 TaskCreated hook supports exit 2 rollback + stderr feedback
PreToolUse hooks return decision via hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision (NOT top-level); top-level 'decision' field DEPRECATED; supports allow/deny/ask/defer with precedence deny>defer>ask>allow
Plugin manifest monitors field auto-arms Monitor streams at session start or skill invoke
Plugin-shipped agents CANNOT define hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode (security restriction). Hooks must be at plugin-level hooks/hooks.json
${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} persists across plugin updates; ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} does NOT
Default plugin hook location is hooks/hooks.json at plugin root; same schema as user-defined hooks
Default monitors location is monitors/monitors.json when path not specified
createEditBatch<OntologyEdit>(client) returns batch with update/create/delete/link/unlink/getEdits methods
Within function execution, reading a property after batch.update does NOT reflect the uncommitted change
Edit functions MUST be wrapped as function-backed Actions to have edits applied
proper-lockfile uses mkdir-based atomic locking — works on NFS and all filesystems
proper-lockfile should work under Bun because it uses standard Node fs APIs (graceful-fs)

contradictions.description: proper-lockfile last published 2020 (5 years old, aging) vs current need for production-proven library
contradictions.resolution: Accept but flag for BackwardProp review. 9.27M weekly downloads confirms production use. Alternative: zero-dep mkdir fallback.

contradictions.description: Plugin-shipped agents CANNOT define hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode (per plugins-reference), but palantir-mini-blueprint.md architecture initially implied agents use hooks field
contradictions.resolution: Define hooks at plugin level (hooks/hooks.json) with matchers targeting agent context (e.g., SubagentStop matcher 'ontology-verifier'), not agent-level frontmatter. Blueprint updated with Correction 1.

contradictions.description: PreToolUse top-level 'decision' field is DEPRECATED in v2.1.108. Deprecated values 'approve'->'allow', 'block'->'deny'. Must use hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision.
contradictions.resolution: All palantir-mini hook code must use new format. Other events (PostToolUse, Stop) continue using top-level decision. Per-event format awareness required. Blueprint updated with Correction 2.

contradictions.description: Foundry edit functions don't apply outside of an Action vs Native Runtime has no Palantir backend. How does palantir-mini commit_edits MCP tool synthesize Action wrapper?
contradictions.resolution: palantir-mini action-executor.md agent + lib/actions/commit.ts atomic writer IS the Action equivalent. The submission_criteria + atomic_commit pattern is adapter-inferred. Flag for ontologist.

contradictions.description: CC Agent Teams 'no nested teams' vs ontology-verifier as a custom subagent spawned from within Agent Teams pipeline
contradictions.resolution: ontology-verifier is a custom subagent, not a nested team. Agent Teams limitation applies to TeamCreate nesting only.

contradictions.description: StaleObject optimistic concurrency (in-place mutation) vs append-only event log (no in-place mutation). How to reject concurrent edits?
contradictions.resolution: Use sequence number in EventEnvelope as optimistic version. Writers check headSequence before append. Flag for prototyper validation.

eventEnvelopeSchema.description: TypeScript discriminated union with 10 event type variants (full code in palantir-mini-blueprint.md Gap fill 1)
baseFields: sequence (monotonic counter — serves as optimistic version)
eventEnvelopeSchema.provenance: Composed from: decision-lineage.md §DL-02 (5D base), typescript-v2-ontology-edits (Edits union), mutations.md §ACTION.MU-14..18 (submission criteria constraint types), validation/README.md §VAL.R-02..R-08 (6 phases)

hookHandlerBodies.preEditOntology: PreToolUse(Edit|Write|MultiEdit) + ontology/*.ts path filter → proper-lockfile on events.jsonl → append 'edit_proposed' event → release → hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext with recent 5-event lineage summary. Uses v2.1.108 hookSpecificOutput format.
hookHandlerBodies.preCompactState: PreCompact → checkOntologyInvariants(cwd) → if fails, output JSON {decision:'block', reason: '...'}. Caveat: blocking auto-compact surfaces underlying context error; safer as advisory for manual trigger.
hookHandlerBodies.taskCompletedGate: TaskCompleted → extract phaseTag from [TAG] prefix regex → runPhaseGates(phaseTag, cwd) → if fails, exit 2 with stderr → else emit 'phase_completed' event. Works with kosmos-registry.json.

atomicAppendMechanism.primary: proper-lockfile (npm, 9.27M dl/week, mkdir-based NFS-safe atomic locking). API: lockfile.lock(path, {stale: 10000, retries: 5}) → release function.
atomicAppendMechanism.fallback: Zero-dep: fs.mkdir(lockDir, {recursive: false}) throws EEXIST atomically. Retry with exponential backoff (50ms base, 2^attempt). Graceful cleanup via try/finally rmdir.
atomicAppendMechanism.caveat: Bun compatibility inferred from standard fs API usage, must be empirically verified in prototype phase (hypothesis A PoC)

migrationSequence: Step 1: Extract schema.ts (2.5KB) first — validate typegen primitives work, minimal blast radius
Step 2: Extract security.ts (4KB) — minimal risk, exercises policy codegen
Step 3: Refactor data.ts (141KB) by ObjectType into primitives/ — requires OSDK codegen ready
Step 4: Migrate capabilities.ts (57KB) to ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/policies/rbac.ts — cross-project shared
Step 5: Migrate changeContracts.ts (66KB) to lib/actions/tier1-declarative.ts + tier2-function.ts
Step 6: Wire events.jsonl emission via plugin hooks/hooks.json (NOT agent frontmatter — per Correction 1)
Step 7: Parallel old-path support via compat shim (re-export from generated files) during transition

gapsForOntologist: StaleObject optimistic concurrency in append-only log model — how to reject concurrent edits without in-place target (hint: sequence number as version vector)
K-LLM consensus implementation — deferred per palantir-mini-blueprint.md open questions
Workflow Lineage vs Decision Lineage product composition — ATOP_WHICH dimension not fully captured beyond git SHA
Edit function rollback semantics — in append-only, rollback = append compensating event (never delete)
Bun compatibility for proper-lockfile — inferred, needs empirical test in prototype phase
Multi-writer cross-project coordination — 3 projects sharing ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ needs coordination

v2108FeatureAvailability.status: All required v2.1.108 features exist — blueprint is feature-complete

nextStep: ontologist spawned for T4. Primary inputs: this source-map.json + palantir-mini-blueprint.md + decision-log.json. Must address 6 gapsForOntologist.
